steps.title: Identify what connects the elements
steps.content: The noun phrase “highest melting point” must link to “all the elements on the periodic table” to show comparison. This requires the preposition “of” to form the standard phrase: “highest melting point of [comparison group].”

steps.title: Apply the preposition rule
steps.content: A preposition connects directly to the noun or noun phrase that follows it without intervening punctuation. Choice A supplies “of” with no extra marks between the preposition and its object “all the elements,” preserving the correct structure. Choices B, C, and D each insert an unnecessary mark (em dash, comma, or colon) that would separate the preposition from its object, violating standard usage. This matches choice A — A is correct.
